title: Dog Vomiting Green Substance: Emergency Triage and Causes
meta_desc: Understand why your dog is vomiting green, when to seek emergency care, and how to help your vet. Urgency Level: High.
content_html:
What is it?

Green vomit is typically a sign that your dog is expelling bile, a digestive fluid produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. Bile is released into the small intestine to aid in digestion, but it can back up into the stomach, causing irritation and vomiting. In other cases, the green color may simply be a result of your dog eating grass or plants.

Is it an emergency?

Urgency Level: High. You should treat green vomiting as a potential emergency. While it can occur on an empty stomach, it is also a primary symptom of serious conditions such as gastrointestinal blockages, heatstroke, or toxic ingestion. If your dog is also showing signs of lethargy, abdominal pain, or repeated retching, please contact an emergency veterinarian immediately.

Why a photo helps triage

It is highly recommended that you take a clear photo of the vomit before cleaning it up. This visual information allows your veterinary triage team to assess the consistency, the specific shade of green, and the presence of any foreign materials or blood. Providing a photo can significantly speed up the diagnostic process and help the medical team determine the severity of your dog's condition.

faqs.question: Can grass make my dog's vomit look green?
faqs.answer: Yes, if your dog consumes a large amount of grass, the chlorophyll will stain the gastric fluid green.

faqs.question: What if my dog is acting normal otherwise?
faqs.answer: Even if your dog seems okay, persistent green vomiting is abnormal. It is best to call your vet to rule out underlying issues like pancreatitis or early-stage obstruction.

faqs.question: Should I feed my dog after they vomit green?
faqs.answer: Do not offer food until you have spoken with a professional. Feeding a dog with a potential blockage or upset stomach can worsen the situation.
